claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child - Alex, who is 14 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 14 years old and lives with you, that's a good start.

To continue assessing your eligibility, could you tell me: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific circumstances where you may still be eligible for Child Benefit.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in, and whether you or your partner are a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in France at the moment. No, neither my partner nor I are Crown servants - we're not diplomats or in the armed forces or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since you're living in France and are not a Crown servant, I need to understand your circumstances a bit more.

Are you an EEA or Swiss national, or do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I'm not an EEA or Swiss national.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me assess your eligibility based on what you've told me.

Based on the Child Benefit rules, to normally qualify for Child Benefit, you must live in the UK. There are exceptions for people living abroad, but these apply mainly to:
- Crown servants (diplomats, armed forces personnel posted abroad)
- Certain EEA or Swiss nationals with specific statuses

Since you're living in France, are not a Crown servant, and don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme or EEA/Swiss national status, you would not meet the residence requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time. 

While Alex is 14 years old and lives with you (which would normally meet the requirements), the main issue is that you're living in France and don't meet the UK residence requirement. Child Benefit generally requires you to live in the UK, and the exceptions for living abroad only apply to specific situations such as:
- Being a Crown servant (diplomat or armed forces personnel)
- Certain EEA or Swiss nationals with specific immigration statuses

Since you don't fall into any of these exception categories, you wouldn't qualify for Child Benefit while living abroad.

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you move back to the UK—you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that time.

reasonings: Alex is 14 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally satisfy the age requirement (under 16) and responsibility test. However, the claimant is living in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that allow Child Benefit claims from abroad. The claimant is not a Crown servant, not an EEA or Swiss national, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant does not meet the UK residence requirement and does not fall under any of the specified exceptions for living abroad.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 14), who lives with them. While the claimant is responsible for a child under 16, they are currently living in France and do not meet the UK residence requirement. They are not a Crown servant and do not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme or EEA/Swiss national status that would allow them to claim while living abroad. Therefore, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant does not meet the UK residence requirement and does not fall under any of the specified exceptions for living abroad.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 14), who lives with them. While the claimant is responsible for a child under 16, they are currently living in France and do not meet the UK residence requirement. They are not a Crown servant and do not have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme or EEA/Swiss national status that would allow them to claim while living abroad. Therefore, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
